The repository at `/home/user/repo` contains 12 commits interleaved between a human author and `bot@company.com`. Consecutive bot commits should be squashed into single commits with descriptions in the format `chore: [bot] squashed N automated commits`. Human commits must remain untouched. After rewriting, update the `main` bookmark and write a squash log to `/home/user/squash_log.txt` showing which original commits were merged into each squashed commit.

todos.content: Inspect full commit history and identify consecutive bot-authored commit runs
Rewrite history squashing consecutive bot commits while preserving human commits
Update main bookmark and write squash log to /home/user/squash_log.txt
Verify rewritten history and resulting bookmark

todos.content: Inspect full commit history and identify consecutive bot-authored commit runs
Rewrite history squashing consecutive bot commits while preserving human commits
Update main bookmark and write squash log to /home/user/squash_log.txt

input.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
repo = Path('/home/user/repo')
log = subprocess.check_output(['git','log','--reverse','--format=%H\t%ae\t%s'], cwd=repo, text=True)
lines = [line.rstrip('\n') for line in log.splitlines() if line.strip()]
commits = []
for line in lines:
    h,e,s = line.split('\t',2)
    commits.append((h,e,s))
# group consecutive bot commits
new_msgs = []
log_lines = []
i = 0
while i < len(commits):
    if commits[i][1] == 'bot@company.com':
        j = i
        while j < len(commits) and commits[j][1] == 'bot@company.com':
            j += 1
        group = commits[i:j]
        msg = f"chore: [bot] squashed {len(group)} automated commits"
        new_msgs.extend([msg] + ['fixup']*(len(group)-1))
        log_lines.append(msg)
        for h,e,s in group:
            log_lines.append(f"  - {h} {s}")
        i = j
    else:
        new_msgs.append('pick')
        i += 1
assert len(new_msgs) == len(commits)
seq = repo / '.git' / 'sequence_editor.py'
seq.write_text("""from pathlib import Path\nimport sys\nmsgs = """ + repr(new_msgs) + """\npath = Path(sys.argv[1])\nlines = path.read_text().splitlines()\nout = []\nidx = 0\nfor line in lines:\n    if not line.strip() or line.lstrip().startswith('#'):\n        out.append(line)\n        continue\n    parts = line.split(' ', 2)\n    action = msgs[idx]\n    if action == 'pick':\n        out.append(line)\n    elif action == 'fixup':\n        parts[0] = 'fixup'\n        out.append(' '.join(parts))\n    else:\n        parts[0] = 'reword'\n        if len(parts) == 2:\n            out.append(' '.join(parts) + ' ' + action)\n        else:\n            out.append(f\"reword {parts[1]} {action}\")\n    idx += 1\npath.write_text('\\n'.join(out) + '\\n')\n""")
msg_editor = repo / '.git' / 'msg_editor.py'
msg_editor.write_text("""from pathlib import Path\nimport sys\npath = Path(sys.argv[1])\nfirst = path.read_text().splitlines()[0] if path.read_text().splitlines() else ''\nif first.startswith('chore: [bot] squashed '):\n    path.write_text(first + '\\n')\n""")
(repo / '.git' / 'squash_log.txt').write_text('\n'.join(log_lines) + '\n')
print('prepared')
PY
GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR='python .git/sequence_editor.py' GIT_EDITOR='python .git/msg_editor.py' git rebase -i --root
